title: Black timer text and End Turn button

requirements.summary: Update GameTimer text styling to render all timer text in black color regardless of warning/critical state transitions
acceptanceCriteria: Match countdown text is black at all times during normal gameplay.
Conquest countdown text is black at all times, including when the conquest countdown is active.
Warning and critical visual states (orange/red) do not override the text color to a non-black value.
Timer remains legible against the parchment background.

file_operations.description: Update the timer text styling so that both match countdown and conquest countdown labels and time values always render in black color. Ensure warning (orange) and critical (red) pulsing states only affect background or border indicators, not the text color itself. The text should remain black and legible against the parchment background at all times.

requirements.summary: Add an 'End Turn' button to the game HUD that allows human players to conclude their turn and advance to the next player
acceptanceCriteria: An 'End Turn' button is visible on the game screen during a human player's turn.
Clicking 'End Turn' advances the turn to the next player.
The button is disabled or hidden when it is a bot's turn or during movement/action animations.
The button is styled with the existing parchment/period theme (earthy colors, period typography).
Bot turns continue to execute automatically without requiring the button.

file_operations.description: Add an 'End Turn' button to the game HUD that is prominently visible during gameplay. The button should be styled consistently with the 16th-century parchment theme using the existing color palette (parchment, terracotta, etc.) and period fonts (Cinzel or IM Fell English). When clicked by a human player, the button should call the turn-advance logic from useGameEngine to conclude the current player's turn and move to the next player. The button must be disabled or hidden when it is not the local human player's turn (e.g., during bot turns or animations). Position the button in a convenient location within the existing HUD layout near other game controls.

Expose a function (e.g., endTurn or advanceTurn) that can be called by the End Turn button to programmatically conclude the current player's turn and advance to the next player. This function should handle all necessary state transitions including clearing selections, updating the current player index, and triggering bot AI if the next player is a bot. Ensure the function validates that it is the human player's turn before allowing the turn to advance.
